reasoning:
The current value of the ICE BofA AAA Corporate Index OAS (as of Nov 21) is 0.37, which will effectively serve as the Nov 23 baseline. 
For Nov 30 (1 week out): The spread recently peaked at 0.38 in early Nov and has settled at 0.36-0.37 over the last two weeks. Because the series is recorded at integer basis points (0.01% intervals), the probability of staying exactly at 0.37 or drifting back to 0.36 is quite high over a short 5-trading-day window. To resolve YES, it must hit >= 0.38. Given the lack of recent upward momentum past 0.37, I estimate P(increase) at ~31%.
For Dec 23 (1 month out): Variance over one month is higher, shrinking the chance of an exact tie. While recent tight spreads present a possibility of reversion toward widening, the tie probability (~10-14%) keeps the strict-increase probability below 50%. I set it to 44%.
For Feb 21 (3 months out): Historically, Q1 2025 saw spreads above 0.40, indicating seasonal or cyclical room for widening from today's relatively tight 0.37 level. Over 3 months, the tie-probability is small. The mild expectation of widening credit conditions (highlighted by recent news on heavy corporate debt supply) tips the balance slightly towards an increase above baseline. Thus, P(increase) is slightly above 0.5, set to 53%.
The uncertainty successfully increases with horizon as requested, with distances from 0.50 being 0.19, 0.06, and 0.03 respectively.
